spec_match.rationale: The concept now uses a real multi-column container with column-width, column-gap, column-fill, overflow, and ::column rules that target generated fragments rather than grid tracks.

interactive.rationale: Mode buttons, width and gap sliders, scroll controls, a CSS readout, and a no-op probe all update the live rendered multicol surface.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature index and concept lede now describe a flat multicol task stream, generated fragments, and the post-layout styling boundary instead of claiming grid-track support.

edge_cases.rationale: The page feature-detects selector(::column), keeps readable fallback content, and includes a measured probe showing width, margin, and font-size declarations on ::column do not reshape the generated layout.

references.rationale: The page links to chromestatus.com/feature/5192332683771904 and the CSS Overflow Level 5 ::column section.

design_system.rationale: The rewritten page uses shared design-system variables and color-mix from those variables, sets button text colors when backgrounds change, and removes inline onclick handlers.

summary: The broken grid-based kanban demo was rebuilt as a real multi-column task-board stream. It now demonstrates ::column on generated fragments, shows supported post-layout styling, includes a no-op layout-property probe, and clears the original critique items.
